text:
Synthetic chemists have to develop ad-hoc synthesis routes to access the complex molecular frameworks of valuable natural products (NP). Intramolecular divergent cyclization of a common precursor yielding a diverse range of structural outcomes is a common theme in many biological pathways and allows for an efficient generation of NP varieties. Here we describe an artificial divergent route using intramolecular divergent cyclizations of a simple common precursor allowing for the access to an enormous diversity of structures related to Terpenoid Indole Alkaloids.

Our strategy allows us to obtain in practice multipotential precursors, from which we can generate, systematically and without losing complexity, a diversity of structures differing by the skeleton, stereochemistry and functionality. Access to the 5 different skeletons including iboga-, aspidosperma-, andranginine-, and ngouniensine-types via three distinct [4+2] cyclizations and two types of redox annulations is achieved from tryptamine in 6–9 steps. In addition, total syntheses of (±)-vincadifformine, (±)-andranginine and (−)-catharanthine were also accomplished. Due to the fact that natural products offer stable and densely packed sp3-congurated architectures which allow their target molecules to interact efficiently by means of multivalency, we believe that our method is particularly pertinent for the preparation of hit Natural Product-like Library (NP-Library) of high quality for use in a drug discovery program.
